subject: Physics
title: Infrasound: Below Human Hearing
description: A quick win: understand Infrasound: Below Human Hearing.

hook.text: You can't hear infrasound, but your body can feel it. It may explain why some places feel 'haunted.'

buildup.text: Infrasound is any sound below 20 Hz. It's produced by earthquakes, volcanoes, and even wind.

discovery.text: At certain frequencies, infrasound causes unease, dizziness, and even visual disturbances in humans.

twist.text: A researcher found that a 'haunted' lab had a fan emitting 18.9 Hz, causing staff to feel dread and see shadows.

climax.text: Monitoring stations detect nuclear tests by their infrasound signatures from thousands of kilometers away.

punchline.text: Can't hear it. Can feel it. Infrasound is the ghost frequency.

quiz.question: What frequency range is classified as infrasound?
